steps.title: Identify the subject and passive voice structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: “its similarity”... ______ evaluated. The subject is similarity (singular), and this is a passive voice construction that requires the subject plus a form of be plus the past participle.

steps.title: Apply singular, present-tense agreement
steps.content: Is (D) is the singular, present-tense form of be that agrees with “similarity” and matches the context (“When a soil is classified...”). The other choices are either plural (A, C) or past tense (B). That matches choice D — D is correct.
